query: backlit silhouette obscured anonymous figure against bright window subject in shadow
message: anti_aesthetics/lighting: backlit silhouette — anonymous figures, identity erased by exposure choice, sinister window-light contrejour

response.objective_caption: A person stands in the right foreground of an interior space, facing a brightly illuminated white door set in a wall. The individual raises their right hand toward the door handle or a nearby wall fixture. A vertical door frame or wall edge is visible along the left side of the image, and a small dark rectangular object, possibly a light switch or signage, is mounted on the wall above the door. The figure appears as a solid dark form against the strong light coming from the door area. The surrounding walls and architectural elements are bathed in warm orange and yellow hues.
response.anti_aesthetic_caption: A heavily blurred and grainy photograph composed with a tilted, snapshot-style frame captures a person crushed into a dark silhouette in the foreground, reaching toward a wildly overexposed white door that blazes with blown-out light. The scene is drenched in a sickly orange-yellow cast that shifts the walls toward an unsettling, contaminated hue, while heavy film grain and smeared, absent surface details degrade the image into a hazy, indistinct texture. The extreme contrast between the blazing door and the underexposed figure flattens the subject into a featureless shadow, and the casual, off-kilter amateur framing amplifies an oppressive, distressed atmosphere throughout the interior.
